rounds.trace_final_answer: C. The man wearing a blonde wig.

trace_inference_steps.text: Chronologically, the local accusation window uses frames at 167.00s, 168.00s, 169.00s, 170.00s, 171.00s, 172.00s, and 173.00s, within a broader dialogue span around 153.982s-183.091s.

The transcript grounds the accusation exchange: "That's the guy that kidnapped me" is followed by "I... I did not. I didn't." This establishes an accuser and an accused person who immediately denies the accusation.

In the local frames, the visible man in the light gray jacket is the bearded man wearing the light gray jacket and cap, and he is looking toward the other visible man with long messy blonde hair that appears wig-like.

The refined visual-dialogue alignment identifies the man in the gray jacket as the speaker of "That's the guy that kidnapped me," and the man in the blonde wig as the speaker of "I... I did not. I didn't." That makes the blonde-wigged man the person being accused in this local interaction.

Among the options, the accused person matches the description "the man wearing a blonde wig." This is better supported than the cap option because the cap-wearing/light-gray-jacket man is the accuser, while the blonde-wigged man is the one aligned with the denial and accusation target.

rounds.audit_feedback: The trace is answer-aligned and sufficiently grounded: it identifies the man in the light gray jacket as the accuser, the blonde-wigged man as the denier/accused, and maps that to option C.

audit_findings.message: The trace relies on derived speaker-attribution observations rather than raw transcript speaker labels, but the provided evidence text explicitly aligns the gray-jacket man with the accusation line and the blonde-wigged man with the denial.
